strengths: Real-time, high-throughput distributed systems experience at Dunnhumby handling millions of HTTP requests per second with low-latency, async architecture
Recent hands-on AI-agent infrastructure work at re:cinq including MCP code generators and agentic workflow frameworks
Long-running event-driven platform ownership (GISCollective) demonstrating architectural persistence and open-source engineering depth
concerns: No evidence of people management or team leadership at any scale — the role requires scaling an org from 10 to 30+ engineers and building a senior technical leadership layer
AI/agent experience is shallow and very recent (current role only); no demonstrated production multi-agent systems with financial or operational consequences
No meaningful fintech-infrastructure depth — credit systems, working-capital allocation, real-time risk, and payment flows are absent from the entire career history

key_factors.factor: No production agentic AI at scale

response.justification: The candidate is a technically capable individual contributor with solid distributed-systems fundamentals and nascent AI-agent exposure, but the profile is misaligned with the CTO role on every leadership and domain dimension: there is no record of managing engineers, owning an engineering organization, setting technical strategy at the executive level, communicating roadmaps to boards, or operating AI systems in regulated fintech environments. The gap between Staff Software Engineer and Chief Technology Officer is substantial and unsupported by the resume.
